Patch Set 6: Code-Review-1

(27 comments)

Needs rebase.

I have some concerns about xattr caching here. I see that in some cases we do not revoke MDS_INODELOCK_XATTR, for example for mnew in mdt_reint_rename() and for the child in mdt_reint_unlink(). Maybe it's OK. Can you check this and add some sanity testing?
Uploaded patch set 7.

Added more test cases, including tests of xattr caching.

Patch Set 8:

The test 164 inytroduced by this patch failed with the following trace:
 striped dir -i0 -c2 /mnt/lustre/d164.sanity/foo1
 striped dir -i0 -c2 /mnt/lustre/d164.sanity/foo2
 ln: creating hard link `/mnt/lustre/d164.sanity/foo2/link' =>  `/mnt/lustre/d164.sanity/foo1/f164.sanity': Invalid cross-device link

The test don't not explicitely stripe dirs on 2 MDS, but it uses test_mkdir to create its directories. Shouldn't it do this?
The failure looks DNE related. Is it expected that this 'ln' operation is not permitted?
Uploaded patch set 9.

(1 comment)

If I do llapi_path2parent("/mnt/lustre/llapi_fid_test_name_9585766", ...) then the returned path is "llapi_fid_test_name_9585766", which is not the parent.


Running the same command above, but setting the name_size parameter to 40, ioctl() will return an overflow error, although there is plenty of room.

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.5/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/f8b046ca-58d4-11e4-a623-5254006e85c2).  Ran 16 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 16:

(2 comments)

"""
If I do llapi_path2parent("/mnt/lustre/llapi_fid_test_name_9585766", ...) then the returned path is "llapi_fid_test_name_9585766", which is not the parent.
"""

Just to clarify: this should display the <parent_fid>/<name> tuples (in your case: [0x200000007:0x1:0x0]/llapi_fid_test_name_9585766) isn't this what you get? The sanity test we added explicitly checks that and seem to work as expected both on my test systems and in maloo...

Patch Set 16:

(2 comments)

Patch Set 16: Code-Review+1

(2 comments)

I'm testing the API, not lfs. The comments make it confusing as to what is supposed to be in the name. I'm looking forward for this API to be integrated.

Is there a plan to update create_restore_volatile() to use that function?

Patch Set 16: Code-Review+1

(1 comment)

Patch Set 16: Code-Review+2

(3 comments)

I wonder if we can also tie this into NFS parent lookup code somehow to make it less expensive.
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as b6e3612d845cf506e977f49aa85ddd1e709fd194

parents.subject: LU-5519 lfsck: repair master LMV for striped directory

commit.subject: LU-3613 llite: Add ioctl to get parent fids from link EA.
commit.message:
LU-3613 llite: Add ioctl to get parent fids from link EA.

Added LL_IOC_GETPARENT to retrieve the <parent_fid>/name(s) of a given
entry, based on its link EA. This saves multiple calls to
path2fid/fid2path.

Added llapi_path2parent() and llapi_fd2parent() wrappers.

Added '--parents' option to lfs path2fid to call this function.

New sanity test 154f to validate the behavior.

Signed-off-by: Thomas Leibovici <thomas.leibovici@cea.fr>
Signed-off-by: Henri Doreau <henri.doreau@cea.fr>
Change-Id: I10f9316da605050be8fc709fe88d87abe86e22a4
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/7069
Tested-by: Jenkins
Reviewed-by: Ned Bass <bass6@llnl.gov>
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: frank zago <fzago@cray.com>
Reviewed-by: John L. Hammond <john.hammond@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
